pages.raw_text:
According to information from the
general offices of the Great Northern
railroad in this city. President j. J..
Hill of that road will leave St. Paul
for western points on his road Tuesday
next. He will arrive in St. Paul from
the east tomorrow or Monday and will
start west Immediately. His visit to
Spokane and the coast is much discuss
ed about the local railroad offices but
its purposes is not known.


After the Spokane shippers have
ANONYMOUSLY retreated and the jobbers
have betrayed the small retailers along
comes Stubs, Harriman's main man,
and now comes j. j. Hill himself.
Those who are supposed to know say
great possibilities lay in those visits
and the coming election, had Spokane
shippers remained true to themselves
and to their city's interests.
